  • [ - ] JSOneHundred - member since 04-Dec-22

    I think it would be good to abolish penalties in the final. Have golden goal from the first minute maybe. If it's drawn then the host gets the cup. The problem with penalties is it's a lottery. Martinez was a bellend in it and the referee was too scared to red card him. Either way, in my opinion a draw at any stage should knock you out in the knockout phase. The team that would have met either side gets a bye. If the final is penalties then it's the host's trophy. That's just my opinion. The standard of officiating in this game was atrocious and will be hard to top for the worst I've ever seen. France got robbed.

    3 years ago

  • [ - ] Icarax - member since 27-Sep-17

    @JSOneHundred i looked at a lot of it and prime messi had better shooting, dribbling and passing lmao. current messi still has better shooting and passing than prime pele. i didnt once see pelé do a 30-yard pass over the top, which messi does every game, i didnt once see pelé dribble 5 players starting from the midfield line and score as messi did several times, and i didnt see pelé score accurate long range bangers as much as messi does. those are the facts whether you like it or not, of course in pelé's era the ball was much heavier so long distance stuff was significantly harder, but those highlights you boomers send us to watch are undoubtedly inferior to messis (though still 2nd in history) and carried by emotions. only thing that might help continue the delusion is saying that most of pelé's highlights werent recorded

  • [ - ] Icarax - member since 27-Sep-17

    i think - technique, passing, midfield aggression won. as such, football won, and a good performance at that was the only way argentina could, and have only ever (aside from 2 magicians), been able to compete with the more developed countries and all the talent they've got. france - blessed with all the talent, genes, fast, tall, even lesser rated players like kolo muani could bully the experienced argentina centrebacks. for france it was about the moments. you have a player in mbappe that can score 3 goals from 3 shots and thats how they came so close to winning, but seeing as argentina were more all-rounded, having much better collective play for most of the final, whereas france got stuck on individual brilliance (dribbles/runs) mostly, you have to say the better team won deservedly. argentina arguably the most all-rounded team of the tournament, and aside from a few moments when the defenders were toasted by frances beasts, they had WC winner level collective play.

    3 years ago
     +7 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us

  • [ - ] Icarax - member since 27-Sep-17

    similar teams. Not so cohesive at times, yet highly efficient at generating smth, with star power & individual brilliance up front, having scored a similar amount of goals throughout the tournament. Defensive stats are also similar (number of goals conceded), both teams have warriors protecting the goal, though I think the gulp in quality & pace between France & Argentina defenders was apparent in the last semi, Argentina can compete through their spirit & aggressive tactics. The fullback positions for Argentina are specifically questioned as they are no-names (exaggeration) coming up against Mbappe & co. France have 4 star forwards being played, that can easily combine, play each other through, cross etc, as an ESPN pundit says, they seem to have more weapons than Argentina, also harder to navigate thru the middle imo. Arg rely on Messis individuality more, tho players like Enzo & MacAllister are very sharp with their passing, defensive duties & decisions all over the pitch. 2-1/1-2

    3 years ago
